mappings.evidence_note: Title page states 'Diploma Programme Chemistry guide, Published February 2023, First assessment 2025'.

Nine aims covering conceptual understanding, knowledge/methods/tools, analysis/evaluation/synthesis, creativity/resilience, designing/modelling solutions, possibilities/limitations of science, technology skills, communication/collaboration, ethical/environmental/economic/cultural/social impact.
AO1 Demonstrate knowledge of: terminology, facts and concepts; skills, techniques and methodologies.
AO2 Understand and apply knowledge of: terminology and concepts; skills, techniques and methodologies.
AO3 Analyse, evaluate, and synthesize: experimental procedures; primary and secondary data; trends, patterns and predictions.
AO4 Demonstrate the application of skills necessary to carry out insightful and ethical investigations.
AO1-AO3 assessed in Paper 1 and Paper 2; AO1-AO4 assessed in scientific investigation (internal assessment). Paper 1 and Paper 2 each approximately 50% AO1+AO2 and 50% AO3.

Syllabus content: Structure 1-3 and Reactivity 1-3. SL total teaching hours 150; HL 240. Experimental programme: practical work 20/40h, collaborative sciences project 10h, scientific investigation 10h.
Roadmap via structure and reactivity; Structure determines reactivity, which transforms structure.
Explicit SL/HL/AHL boundary table extracted to sl-hl-ahl-boundaries.json. Each sub-topic has 'Standard level and higher level: N hours' and optional 'Additional higher level: N hours' (or 'None').
Structure 1 Models of the particulate nature of matter. 1.1 SL/HL only (2h); 1.2 SL/HL (2h) + AHL (1h); 1.3 SL/HL (3h) + AHL (3h); 1.4 SL/HL only (7h); 1.5 SL/HL only (3h).
Structure 2 Models of bonding and structure. 2.1 SL/HL only (4h); 2.2 SL/HL (10h) + AHL (8h); 2.3 SL/HL (2h) + AHL (1h); 2.4 SL/HL (4h) + AHL (1h).
Structure 3 Classification of matter. 3.1 SL/HL (7h) + AHL (4h); 3.2 SL/HL (9h) + AHL (11h).
Reactivity 1 What drives chemical reactions?. 1.1 SL/HL only (5h); 1.2 SL/HL (3h) + AHL (5h); 1.3 SL/HL only (4h); 1.4 AHL only (5h).
Reactivity 2 How much, how fast and how far?. 2.1 SL/HL only (7h); 2.2 SL/HL (9h) + AHL (6h); 2.3 SL/HL (5h) + AHL (4h).
Reactivity 3 What are the mechanisms of chemical change?. 3.1 SL/HL (8h) + AHL (9h); 3.2 SL/HL (10h) + AHL (5h); 3.3 SL/HL only (2h); 3.4 SL/HL (4h) + AHL (7h).

SL: External assessment 3h (80%). Paper 1 1h30m 36% (55 marks: 1A MCQ 30 marks, 1B data-based 25 marks). Paper 2 1h30m 44% (50 marks short-answer/extended-response). Internal assessment 10h 20% (24 marks scientific investigation).

HL: External assessment 4h30m (80%). Paper 1 2h 36% (75 marks: 1A MCQ 40 marks, 1B data-based 35 marks). Paper 2 2h30m 44% (90 marks short-answer/extended-response). Internal assessment 10h 20% (24 marks scientific investigation).
Scientific investigation internally assessed and externally moderated; four criteria: Research design, Data analysis, Conclusion, Evaluation.
Command terms by AO: AO1 Draw/State; AO2 Annotate/Calculate/Describe/Estimate/Outline; AO3 Comment/Compare/Contrast/Deduce/Determine/Discuss/Evaluate/Explain/Predict/Sketch/Suggest.
Clean copy of Chemistry data booklet required for external assessment.

draft_notes: Page numbers use printed/page numbers from the PDF footer.
